questions.question: Should paired-mode merge use equal weight per run or weight by unique vignette count?
questions.rationale: Each vignette order contributes equally to the combined picture; adding a vignetteCount field is more correct but larger scope

assumptions: count fields stay as raw response counts — only winRate changes to use the two-step approach
aggregate_transcripts_by_model stays unchanged; only build_preference_summary changes for byValue rates
Frontend fix removes the allHaveCounts pooled-sum path and trusts the backend per-vignette winRate
For aggregate multi-run merge, fallback continues to use sampleSize as weight — documented simplification
discovery.summary: Fixed via conversation context. User confirmed equal-weight-per-run for paired merge.
acceptance_criteria: Paired-mode merge averages the two per-run winRates with equal weight

parallel_analysis.note: Slices A and C touch disjoint files and can run in parallel. A: Python worker (analyze_basic_aggregation.py, test_analyze_basic.py). C: Frontend (analysisSemantics.preference.ts, analysisSemantics.test.ts). Slices are defined as ## sections in tasks.md without checkbox format — parallelism will be managed manually at dispatch time. Slice B must follow A.
